message:
feat(tutorial): auto-skip animations via centralized motion detection

The tutorial model now initializes skipAnimations from
styles.ReducedMotionEnabled(), so running inside tmux, screen, CI, or
with NTM_REDUCE_MOTION=1 automatically presents fully-revealed slides
without the typewriter/particle animation sequence.

Specific changes:
- New() sets skipAnimations from ReducedMotionEnabled() instead of
  only setting it when WithSkipAnimations() option is passed.
- Init() returns nil (no tick scheduling) when skipAnimations is true,
  previously it always returned a batch of tick + EnterAltScreen.
  The EnterAltScreen is removed entirely as it conflicts with the
  palette's existing alt-screen management.
- Update(TickMsg) short-circuits when skipAnimations is true, preventing
  animation tick advancement.
- renderSlide() forces tick=1000 when skipAnimations is true, which
  makes all progressive-reveal slide content appear fully rendered
  immediately (the reveal thresholds in each slide's render function
  treat high tick values as "complete").

Test:
- TestNewTutorialModelAutoSkipsAnimationsInTmux verifies skipAnimations
  is true and Init() returns nil when TMUX env var is set.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
